parsed_review.summary: CP00-275 (Risk C, 150 units, RP08.P01.M06.S05–RP08.P02.M02.S22) is a clean descriptor-only pack that closes the RP08.P01 email-dms-core model/storage tail (M06 fixtures, M07–M10 Test/Hermes/Claude/Closeout) and opens the RP08.P02 service foundation (Scope Inventory, Contract Draft, Type And Shape Definition). Source inspection via Read/Grep/Glob across all eight in-scope files (contract JSON, README, registry/service/validators/index, model test, validation script) confirms full alignment on the CP00-275 binding and preservation of every no-write/no-runtime/no-real-data boundary. (1) The pack-level production_ready claim is correctly supported and not over-stated: command-evidence reports production_ready=false with status implementation_and_plan_gates_passed_pending_final_closeout, no_real_data=true, writes_product_state=false, all ~20 local/Hermes commands exit 0, and Hermes gate H08 passed; the binding's production_ready_flag is a descriptor verification string, not an enterprise trust claim, and final closeout remains post-review human-gated. (2) CP178+ review hardening holds: readiness auth_preflight passed (exit 0, 1445-byte valid JSON, zero errors/warnings), no zero-byte/malformed/fenced/tool-call-shaped evidence, forbidden_review_evidence enforced in the registry, and validation-report confirms forbidden_tools_present=false with read-tool source inspection (summary-only prohibited for this Risk C / security-audit / review-evidence pack). (3) Claude authority boundary preserved: authority_boundary objects across service, validators, and tests assert claude_is_final_approval=false, local_validation_claims_enterprise_trust=false, and human_final_approval_required_for_runtime_opening=true. (4) Hermes patterns are confined to deterministic control-plane review packet/receipt handling (descriptor_only=true, hermes_runtime_embedded_in_product=false), not product runtime execution. No P0/P1/P2/P3 findings; the pack does not block pack or goal closeout.
